beats.sup0: second person; you are {HERO} of {VESSEL} easing out of the last dark slip on the near shore of {STRAIT}; the crossing is one hard route through a line of checkpoints the far side keeps lit and listening; grave stakes, stated plainly: many have gone and few have returned
beats.sup1: the near lights fall away; the strait opens black and wide; the one rule of the run is stated here: there is no turning back and no second boat, only the next mark and the next, all the way across or not at all
beats.st01_ap0: the first mark is {ST01_OBSTACLE}: a chain-and-net barrier across the mouth, its floats riding low, an ebb gap worked into the seaward corner by the tide
beats.st01_pass: past {ST01_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st01_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the screw takes the chain and stops the boat dead on the barrier
beats.st01_f0_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st01_f0_end: the runner hangs dead on the boom in the open while the shore guns find the range; the strait keeps it, quick and without dwelling
beats.st01_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the net takes the bow and will not give it back
beats.st01_f1_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st01_f1_end: the tide swings you broadside against the boom and holds you there; the run ends in the first hour of the crossing
beats.st01_f2_c0: the mistake lands: waiting spends the dark you needed
beats.st01_f2_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st01_f2_end: the patrol closes on a boat that sat too long; the water is cold and the end is fast
beats.st01_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the hull meets steel that was made not to yield
beats.st01_f3_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st01_f3_end: the floats are strung on steel that does not break; the bow stoves and the sea comes in where you struck
beats.st01_f4_c0: the mistake lands: the near end is the watched end
beats.st01_f4_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st01_f4_end: the shoreward end sits under the guardhouse lamps; you are lit the moment you close it, and the wall has all the time it needs
beats.st01_slip: scrape the gap late and take a corner float along the hull; loud, but through
beats.st01_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the loud engine writes your position on the dark
beats.st01_fd_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st01_fd_end: the noise brings the light and then the gun; you do not clear the shore
beats.st01_cap: hands up in the dark; the guard boat comes alongside and the run is over before it began, but you are alive
beats.st01_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST01_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st01_gate: you are on {ST01_OBSTACLE}; it is slipped only through the ebb gap on the seaward corner; the chain fouls a screw and the net holds a bow, and either one pins you under the shore guns; ride the gap, never the barrier; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st02_ap0: {ST02_OBSTACLE} mark the swept water; some ride true and some are false, wired to the mines below and set to look exactly like the rest
beats.st02_pass: past {ST02_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st02_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the shortcut runs straight over the charges
beats.st02_f0_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st02_f0_end: inside the line the bottom is mined; the strait takes the runner and its pilot without ceremony
beats.st02_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the doubled mark was the warning you did not read
beats.st02_f1_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st02_f1_end: the buoy was a lure set over a charge; the water closes over the run
beats.st02_f2_c0: the mistake lands: wide water here is mined water
beats.st02_f2_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st02_f2_end: wide of the swept lane is the field itself; the end comes from below, sudden and final
beats.st02_f3_c0: the mistake lands: there is no reading a mark at speed
beats.st02_f3_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st02_f3_end: speed leaves no time to read a doubled mark from a single one; you read one wrong at the worst moment
beats.st02_f4_c0: the mistake lands: a light tells you nothing true here
beats.st02_f4_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st02_f4_end: the false buoys carry lights too; a light is not a topmark, and the one you trusted sat over the mine
beats.st02_slip: misread one mark and feel the hull kiss a mooring wire; it holds, and you back onto the single line
beats.st02_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the mark you chose sits over the charge
beats.st02_fd_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st02_fd_end: trusting the doubled mark is trusting the mine; there is no reading it twice
beats.st02_cap: the launch was waiting for exactly this; they board in silence and the crossing ends, but not your life
beats.st02_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST02_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st02_gate: you are on {ST02_OBSTACLE}; the true buoys carry a single steady topmark and the false ones a doubled mark; steer buoy to buoy on the single mark and the mined water on either side will not touch you; read every mark before you turn; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st03_ap0: the guard cutter works {ST03_OBSTACLE} across the middle water, up the line and back, turning at each end with her stern to the strait
beats.st03_pass: past {ST03_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st03_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the bow watch has the whole strait to look across
beats.st03_f0_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st03_f0_end: the searchlight and the horn come together; the run ends in the middle of the strait
beats.st03_f1_c0: the mistake lands: there is nowhere to be beside her that is not seen
beats.st03_f1_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st03_f1_end: you cannot hide in a straight line beside a warship; she turns and you are simply there
beats.st03_f2_c0: the mistake lands: stillness on flat water is its own signal
beats.st03_f2_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st03_f2_end: a stopped hull on flat water is a shape against the light; close by is close enough
beats.st03_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the stern is watched as well as the bow
beats.st03_f3_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st03_f3_end: close under the stern is close under the after watch; they look straight down onto you
beats.st03_f4_c0: the mistake lands: she never leaves; only your night does
beats.st03_f4_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st03_f4_end: she does not leave the lane; the dark you spent waiting is gone and the dawn is not far
beats.st03_slip: mistime the turn and take her wash broadside; you wallow unseen by a hand's width and claw into the dark
beats.st03_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the open water is all hers
beats.st03_fd_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st03_fd_end: a runner does not outrun a cutter across open water; her guns reach where your engine cannot
beats.st03_cap: the cutter comes down slow and certain; you are taken off the strait alive, the run finished
beats.st03_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST03_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st03_gate: you are on {ST03_OBSTACLE}; cross astern of her on the turn, in the blind quarter of her stern wake; cross ahead of her bow and her lookouts have the whole open strait to see you against; time the turn and take the blind water; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st04_ap0: you come under {ST04_OBSTACLE}: a shore searchlight beam going over the water and back, holding the far side, swinging across on a slow ten-count, holding, swinging back
beats.st04_pass: past {ST04_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st04_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the beam lays across a boat that did not stop
beats.st04_f0_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st04_f0_end: the guns answer the moving wake; the reach keeps the runner
beats.st04_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the turn makes light of its own
beats.st04_f1_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st04_f1_end: the turn throws a bright curl exactly where the beam is looking; the end is fast and grave
beats.st04_f2_c0: the mistake lands: light crosses the reach faster than any hull
beats.st04_f2_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st04_f2_end: you cannot outrun a beam of light; it lays over you mid-reach
beats.st04_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the lamp sees best closest in
beats.st04_f3_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st04_f3_end: there is no blind spot at the foot of a searchlight; you drive straight into its keepers
beats.st04_f4_c0: the mistake lands: your own light gives you up
beats.st04_f4_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st04_f4_end: a light answered from the water is a boat confessed; they hold the beam on you and fire
beats.st04_slip: freeze a beat too late and feel the beam brush your stern; it moves on unsure and you go still until it is gone
beats.st04_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the wake is all they need
beats.st04_fd_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st04_fd_end: daring a gun line is not a plan; they track the wake and close the reach
beats.st04_cap: you give yourself to the beam and the boat behind it; the reach takes the run but leaves you breathing
beats.st04_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST04_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st04_gate: you are on {ST04_OBSTACLE}; move only while the beam is away and the water is dark; when the bar comes over, freeze and be a still shape among still shapes; motion under the light is what the gunners fire on; run the dark, hold the light; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st05_ap0: the water goes quiet in a way that listens; {ST05_OBSTACLE} on the point hears engines across the whole narrows
beats.st05_pass: past {ST05_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st05_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the note carries the whole width of the water
beats.st05_f0_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st05_f0_end: you are found by sound before you are ever seen; the narrows keep the runner
beats.st05_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the quiet keeps nothing back
beats.st05_f1_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st05_f1_end: one note is enough; the bearing goes out and the end comes up the strait to meet you
beats.st05_f2_c0: the mistake lands: speed is only a louder note
beats.st05_f2_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st05_f2_end: sprinting past a listening post is announcing yourself; the picket is already on the bearing
beats.st05_f3_c0: the mistake lands: reverse thunders where ahead only murmured
beats.st05_f3_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st05_f3_end: a reversed screw is the loudest note of all; the post has you before you have steerage
beats.st05_slip: a gust swings you and you feed her a breath of throttle; the post stirs, uncertain, and you crawl on
beats.st05_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the sound arrives before you do
beats.st05_fd_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st05_fd_end: you cannot outrun a bearing already sent; the picket is on the line ahead of you
beats.st05_cap: you go quiet for good and let the picket find you; they take you off the narrows alive
beats.st05_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST05_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st05_gate: you are on {ST05_OBSTACLE}; drop to a crawl and let the tide do the work; a hard engine note carries straight to the post and lays the picket on your bearing; run silent or run seen; keep the note low; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st06_ap0: {ST06_OBSTACLE} lie ahead: moored charges just under the surface, their float wires catching starlight in thin lines, a single swept drift of clear water down the middle
beats.st06_pass: past {ST06_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st06_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the first wire finds the hull
beats.st06_f0_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st06_f0_end: the wider gap is wide because it is mined; the narrows end the run in a single grave instant
beats.st06_f1_c0: the mistake lands: haste hauls the mine home
beats.st06_f1_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st06_f1_end: speed turns a brushed wire into a struck charge; the end is sudden
beats.st06_f2_c0: the mistake lands: each turn adds a wire to cross
beats.st06_f2_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st06_f2_end: weaving only multiplies the wires you cross; one is enough
beats.st06_f3_c0: the mistake lands: there is no gentle way to move a charge
beats.st06_f3_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st06_f3_end: the float is the mine; to touch it is to fire it
beats.st06_f4_c0: the mistake lands: another's luck is not a chart
beats.st06_f4_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st06_f4_end: the wake you followed was luck, not a lane; where it went safe you go a foot wrong and that foot is the field
beats.st06_slip: a wire slides along the hull and does not catch; you hold your breath and your line to the last float
beats.st06_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the field is wired everywhere but the drift
beats.st06_fd_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st06_fd_end: a minefield does not answer to nerve; the new lane is wired like the rest
beats.st06_cap: the shore sends a pilot that is also a guard; you are led out of the field and off the run, alive
beats.st06_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST06_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st06_gate: you are on {ST06_OBSTACLE}; hold the clear drift dead center where no wires show; a wire touched at speed pulls the charge to the hull; stay off the wires and off the throttle; center and slow is the only water that forgives; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st07_ap0: {ST07_OBSTACLE} lies across the strait: a drowned hull grounded years back, its plating just below the surface at this state of tide
beats.st07_pass: past {ST07_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st07_f0_c0: the mistake lands: calm is only the plating holding still
beats.st07_f0_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st07_f0_end: calm water over a wreck is shallow water; the strait comes in where the plating opened the hull
beats.st07_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the near end is the high end
beats.st07_f1_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st07_f1_end: the bow of the wreck sits highest; you ground hard in the open and the shore lights find you
beats.st07_f2_c0: the mistake lands: iron does not let a keel bounce
beats.st07_f2_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st07_f2_end: you do not skip a wreck; you tear the bottom out on it
beats.st07_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the current lays you on the iron
beats.st07_f3_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st07_f3_end: the current sets you sideways onto the very plating you meant to clear; slow does not mean safe here
beats.st07_slip: the keel grazes plating in the scour and grinds free; you feel the strait not quite take you and steer on
beats.st07_fd_c0: the mistake lands: speed only sharpens the iron
beats.st07_fd_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st07_fd_end: commitment against a wreck only holes you faster; the run ends grounded and torn
beats.st07_cap: you set her on the wreck on purpose and wait; the guard skiff takes you off, the crossing lost, your life kept
beats.st07_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST07_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st07_gate: you are on {ST07_OBSTACLE}; the only water over the wreck is the scour channel off its stern where the tide has dug deep; everywhere else the plating waits a foot down to open your hull; take the stern scour or nothing; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st08_ap0: {ST08_OBSTACLE} ranges the outer water; the far battery's shots walk in slow rows across the swell, each row a little nearer the last, with a fired-over lull between
beats.st08_pass: past {ST08_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st08_f0_c0: the mistake lands: you arrive where the shells are already sent
beats.st08_f0_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st08_f0_end: ahead of the ranging rows is where the next salvo falls; the gun line closes on you
beats.st08_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the rows come back around to your stillness
beats.st08_f1_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st08_f1_end: the guns do not stop for a waiting boat; the rows walk back over you
beats.st08_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the zigzag only lengthens your stay in the fall
beats.st08_f2_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st08_f2_end: a battery ranges an area, not a point; the zigzag keeps you in the beaten zone longer
beats.st08_f3_c0: the mistake lands: speed carries you into the next fall
beats.st08_f3_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st08_f3_end: full power carries you across two rows into the third; you outrun the lull, not the shells
beats.st08_f4_c0: the mistake lands: the turn gives them your length
beats.st08_f4_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st08_f4_end: there is no back on this run; the turn offers your broadside and the end finds you turning
beats.st08_slip: a row falls close enough to throw water over the deck; the boat staggers, holds, and you drive on through the spray
beats.st08_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the fall does not care how steady your hand
beats.st08_fd_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st08_fd_end: nerve does not turn a shell; the next row does not miss twice
beats.st08_cap: the white light stills the battery; a boat comes out and the run is over, but you live
beats.st08_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST08_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st08_gate: you are on {ST08_OBSTACLE}; run in the lull just behind the last row, on water already fired over and not yet fired on again; get ahead of the rows and you put yourself where the next salvo is already aimed; stay behind the fall; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st09_ap0: {ST09_OBSTACLE} lies across the far half of the strait, thick enough to blind the shore and thick enough to blind you
beats.st09_pass: past {ST09_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st09_f0_c0: the mistake lands: feel bends your track back the way you came
beats.st09_f0_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st09_f0_end: you circle back under the battery you cleared and the guns finish the crossing
beats.st09_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the fog hides the hull you strike
beats.st09_f1_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st09_f1_end: blind speed finds the one hard thing in all that softness; the run ends against what you never saw
beats.st09_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the horn is a bearing given away
beats.st09_f2_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st09_f2_end: the echo that answers is a guard boat's; the horn fixed your bearing for the picket as surely as for you
beats.st09_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the tide steers what you will not
beats.st09_f3_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st09_f3_end: the tide inside the fog carries you where you cannot tell; you drift onto the shore batteries broadside
beats.st09_slip: the fog thins for a heartbeat and shows you off course; you correct to the compass and the grey folds back
beats.st09_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the feeling was wrong the whole time
beats.st09_fd_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st09_fd_end: the gut in fog is a liar; it walks you back into the beaten water
beats.st09_cap: the picket under the fog finds the still boat; they take you quietly, the run undone
beats.st09_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST09_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st09_gate: you are on {ST09_OBSTACLE}; the fog hides you only on a held compass course and a slow steady note; chase your own sense of direction inside it and you circle back onto the guns you just passed; trust the needle, not the feeling; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st10_ap0: {ST10_OBSTACLE} of small guard boats rides the inshore swell, spaced wide, each showing a shaded lamp to hold station on the next, a dark gap of unwatched water between them
beats.st10_pass: past {ST10_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st10_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the lamp's own crew sees best right beneath it
beats.st10_f0_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st10_f0_end: they have you at once and the picket line closes; the run ends inshore
beats.st10_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the noise gathers all the lamps
beats.st10_f1_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st10_f1_end: a charge draws every shaded lamp at once; the boats converge on the noise
beats.st10_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the screen turns and looks back
beats.st10_f2_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st10_f2_end: a picket that feels a shadow at its stern turns; there is no hiding behind the boat whose task is to watch
beats.st10_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the line keeps its lights all night
beats.st10_f3_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st10_f3_end: the lamps do not go out; they are relieved, never dark, and your night runs out first
beats.st10_f4_c0: the mistake lands: overlap is the brightest water of all
beats.st10_f4_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st10_f4_end: where two lamps overlap the water is doubly lit, not blind; both crews have you
beats.st10_slip: a lamp swings your way and you go flat and dark; it passes over you and holds on its neighbor and you slide through
beats.st10_fd_c0: the mistake lands: fresh boats run down a tired one
beats.st10_fd_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st10_fd_end: the pickets are fresh and inshore and many; a spent runner does not outrun them home
beats.st10_cap: you go dark and still and let them come; the picket boats close around you in their lamps, alive
beats.st10_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST10_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st10_gate: you are on {ST10_OBSTACLE}; thread the dark gap between two shaded lamps where each picket is night-blind toward the other; pass close under one lamp and its own crew has you in the light; take the gap, not the boat; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st11_ap0: {ST11_OBSTACLE} guards the inner water: a log-and-net line with a warden's skiff patrolling its length on a lantern
beats.st11_pass: past {ST11_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st11_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the lantern lights you for the warden
beats.st11_f0_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st11_f0_end: the lantern shows the warden your hull and the warden shows the shore; the inner water keeps the run
beats.st11_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the logs turn and hold the keel
beats.st11_f1_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st11_f1_end: the logs roll and foul and stop you astride the boom; the skiff comes back to a boat gone still
beats.st11_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the round comes back before the cut is done
beats.st11_f2_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st11_f2_end: the net is longer than your nerve and the skiff's round; the lantern returns before the last strand parts
beats.st11_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the skiff keeps its round past your night
beats.st11_f3_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st11_f3_end: the skiff does not moor before dawn; the reeds hold you until the light does the warden's work
beats.st11_slip: the skiff's lantern lifts your way and you drop behind a boom float; it swings past and you slip the end
beats.st11_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the shout outruns the boat
beats.st11_fd_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st11_fd_end: the warden's shout carries to the inner posts faster than your hull; they are ready inshore
beats.st11_cap: you let the skiff come alongside; the warden takes your line and the run, but not your life
beats.st11_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST11_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st11_gate: you are on {ST11_OBSTACLE}; the warden's skiff works it end to end on a fixed lantern-lit round; slip the boom at the far end just after the skiff turns away, in the dark it leaves behind; meet the lantern and the whole inner shore wakes; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st12_ap0: {ST12_OBSTACLE} lies across the last mile: the swell rearing and breaking over it in the dark, a line of pale collapsing water
beats.st12_pass: past {ST12_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st12_f0_c0: the mistake lands: the set arrives while you are broadside
beats.st12_f0_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st12_f0_end: a breaking sea on the bow stands her up and throws her over; the bar rolls the run under a mile from shore
beats.st12_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the short line is the shallow line
beats.st12_f1_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st12_f1_end: the shallow break is the hardest water on the bar; she broaches and fills
beats.st12_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the break comes apart around the hull, and the hull with it
beats.st12_f2_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st12_f2_end: the bar does not let a boat slam through; it takes the hull apart on the way
beats.st12_f3_c0: the mistake lands: the beam meets the crest and loses
beats.st12_f3_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st12_f3_end: quartering a breaking bar gives the sea your beam at the top of the slot; she goes over sideways
beats.st12_slip: a set catches you half-slotted and the stern lifts hard; you drive off the crest and down the back, shipping water but through
beats.st12_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the water gives the screw nothing to hold
beats.st12_fd_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st12_fd_end: the whitewater has no bottom to push against; the screw races and the sea takes charge
beats.st12_cap: you hold off the break and show a light; a shore boat crosses the bar you could not and takes you in, alive
beats.st12_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST12_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st12_gate: you are on {ST12_OBSTACLE}; cross on the back of a swell through the deep slot where the break is lowest, timed to the sets; take it on the face of a breaking sea and it stands the boat on end and rolls her; read the sets, take the slot; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.st13_ap0: {ST13_OBSTACLE} waits: a low belt of staked wire and obstacles the length of it, with one cleared lane the far side keeps for its own boats, held on a leading mark against the dunes
beats.st13_pass: past {ST13_OBSTACLE} the strait lets you on; the far shore is a little nearer and the run a little longer survived
beats.st13_f0_c0: the mistake lands: nearest is not clearest
beats.st13_f0_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st13_f0_end: the wire holds the boat fast in the wash and the sentries have you against the pale surf; the run ends on the beach
beats.st13_f1_c0: the mistake lands: the obstacles were made for the hard-driven boat
beats.st13_f1_c1: one held breath in which the whole run narrows to this; no gore, only the cold certainty of it arriving
beats.st13_f1_end: the belt is built to stop exactly this; the boat hangs holed on the stakes
beats.st13_f2_c0: the mistake lands: the unexpected place is the well-wired place
beats.st13_f2_c1: there is a moment where it could still be undone, and then that moment is gone; grave, quiet, no cruelty drawn out
beats.st13_f2_end: the flank is wired thickest where boats are least expected; she grounds fast in it
beats.st13_f3_c0: the mistake lands: a guess is a stake's width wrong
beats.st13_f3_c1: the strait is very old and very patient and it has all the time this takes; the fear is clean and brief
beats.st13_f3_end: a guessed mark lands you a stake's width into the belt; a width is enough on this beach
beats.st13_slip: you drift a stake's width off the mark and feel wire drag the hull; it tears free and you steer back onto the lead
beats.st13_fd_c0: the mistake lands: the belt takes the driven boat
beats.st13_fd_c1: you have a heartbeat to know it and no way left to stop it; the register stays grave and spare
beats.st13_fd_end: you cannot drive over a staked belt; the wire holds the boat in the surf until the guns arrive
beats.st13_cap: you rise in the surf with empty hands; the beach party wades out and the run ends in the shallows, alive
beats.st13_seen: you are spotted but still moving off {ST13_OBSTACLE}; one breath to choose; state the three ways this goes and that only the hard quiet one keeps the run alive
beats.st13_gate: you are on {ST13_OBSTACLE}; come in on the leading mark down the cleared lane where the wire is cut; run the beach anywhere else and the obstacles hold the boat fast in the surf under the sentries' guns; find the mark, keep the lane; state the rule plainly before the choice so the reader can see which way is death
beats.land_ap0: the boat noses onto shingle in the dark; the crossing is behind you but the beach is not yet safe; a shape waits up the spit where the contact should be
beats.land_ap1: you kill the engine; the silence after all that noise is enormous; the shape resolves into a figure, then two, and one of them stands wrong
beats.shore_gate: the last rule of the run, stated here: the contact answers a fixed countersign and never moves toward you first; a figure that closes without the word is a sentry; give the word and hold, or you walk the whole strait's work into a gun on dry land
beats.shore_run: you run past the word into the dark and the dark is wired and watched; the beach party takes you a hundred yards from the crossing you survived
beats.shore_shoot: you draw first and are wrong; the figure was your own contact and the shot brings the sentries; the run ends on the beach it should have won
beats.shore_res0: the countersign comes back in a low steady voice; the figure stops, then lifts a hand; the contact is real and the run is nearly done
beats.win_gate: the contact offers two ways off the beach and one truth: you made a crossing few make; state the choice between a clean quiet handoff, a harder faster one under the guns, and the plain spent honest arrival, each a real end to the run
beats.win_clean: the handoff is clean and silent; {CARGO} goes inland into safe hands and you with it, the whole lethal strait behind you and every mark of it survived; a clean run, the rarest kind, told plainly and without triumph
beats.win_costly: the fast route out draws fire and you take it and keep going; you reach the safe line battered and half-swamped but whole, the run made at a price; a costly crossing earned the hard way to the last yard
beats.win_quiet: you give up {CARGO} and sit on the cold shingle with nothing left; the run is done and so are you, and both of those are enough; a quiet arrival, no triumph in it, only the strait crossed and the work delivered
